title: Cave of Awakening
description: Learn the core building blocks: selecting columns, filtering rows, transforming data, and sorting. Every PySpark journey starts here.

theory_scroll.content: Dungeon I covers the five core DataFrame operations every PySpark engineer uses daily: select(), filter(), withColumn(), groupBy().agg(), and orderBy(). Mastering these — and understanding what happens under the hood — is the foundation for everything else in Spark.

rows: Column pruning — read only what you need from Parquet
Use & | ~ not and/or; predicates get pushed to file reader
Avoid chaining 20+ calls — use select() instead
2-phase aggregate: partial (local) then shuffle then final
Global sort = range-partition shuffle. Pair with limit(N)

theory_scroll.content: Run df.explain(mode='formatted') on any query to see what Spark actually plans to do. Learning to read query plans is the single most impactful skill for performance debugging at MAANG.
